title: Blanchy Mexican Steak Stir-fry
description: A sweet and creamy brunch-mood dish featuring lean minced steak, perfectly blanched for a quick, easy meal.

ingredients.name: 1 cup heavy cream
ingredients.notes: Use room temperature to facilitate whisking

ingredients.name: Juice of 1 lime
ingredients.notes: For a citrusy twist on sweetness and creaminess

ingredients.name: 8 eggs
ingredients.notes: Beaten lightly to mix with the stir-fry

instructions: Blanch the flank steak in boiling water for about 1 minute, then immediately rinse under cold water.
In a pan, add olive oil and heat until hot. Add minced garlic and cook until fragrant but not browned.
Add thinly sliced mushrooms to the pan and sauté until they start to soften.
Gently fold in the blanched steak pieces into the mushrooms, stirring them around to coat.
Pour in heavy cream and gently simmer for about 2 minutes until it starts to thicken slightly. Add lime juice to taste.
Beat the eggs lightly and pour over the creamy mixture in the pan. Stir quickly until just combined, avoiding scrambling too much.
Serve immediately with a side of tortillas or warm cornbread for an authentic Mexican brunch vibe.
